what_it_does: Implements a hierarchical image record store for a fabric/ledger system. Images are addressed as image://<channel>/<path>, stored as YAML-frontmatter markdown files in channel-memory/images, while their binary bytes live in a separate content-addressed blob store. It provides save_image (with append-only versioning), get_image (including @v<n> version projection), list_images (tree navigation), list_versions, image_bytes (blob resolution), and list_groups (group counts).
observations: Images are identified by a hierarchical address image://<channel>/<path>, not by a flat global id.
Binary bytes are stored in a content-addressed blob store via store.put_blob / store.get_blob; only metadata records live on disk.
Records are persisted as markdown files with YAML frontmatter, keyed by a safe encoding of the address.
save_image is append-only: re-saving the same address creates a new version rather than overwriting history.
get_image supports versioned addresses like image://channel/path@v2 and projects that specific version onto the returned record.
list_images can be filtered by a channel or collection prefix to navigate the tree at any depth.
MIME types are sniffed from bytes and optionally validated against a declared MIME type.
The module references integration points (attachments, board comments, board references, /api/image route) only in docstrings, not in implemented code.
standouts: Hierarchical, navigable address space for images (channel → collection → name).
Append-only versioning with explicit @v<n> address projection.
Fail-loud semantics: missing images, bad MIME, or malformed records raise ImageError rather than returning empty values.
Separation of address identity from on-disk filename (hierarchical address vs flat safe filename).
conventions: Heavy docstrings that explain design intent and failure modes.
Private helpers prefixed with underscore for rendering, parsing, normalization, MIME sniffing, and filename mapping.
Explicit type hints on public and private functions.
Frontmatter YAML + body format for persisted records.
Use of external contracts (contracts.address, store.fs_store) for address parsing and safe filename generation.
Fail-loud exception class ImageError used consistently.
concerns: list_versions parses the address twice redundantly.
get_image mutates the returned record dict when projecting a specific version, including rewriting the address field.
_split_frontmatter uses simple string slicing rather than a robust YAML frontmatter parser.
_sniff_mime SVG detection could false-positive on arbitrary XML or HTML content.
IMAGES_DIR is created only inside save_image; list_images silently returns [] if the directory is missing.
No concurrency control or locking around read-modify-write of versioned records.
list_groups treats any path with at least two segments as a group, which may misclassify deeply nested leaves if not intended.
notes: A small, opinionated image metadata ledger that treats hierarchical addresses and immutable blobs as the source of truth, with versioning and loud failure modes.
questions: What exact fields does parse_image_address return, and what does image_address / versioned_address produce?
What interface does the blob store expose (put_blob return type, get_blob behavior for missing blobs)?
How are image width and height intended to be supplied when saving generated images?
What is the expected structure and semantics of the links list?
Where are the /api/image/<channel>/<path> route, attachment targeting, and board comment/reference integrations implemented?
Are channels created implicitly by first save, or is there a separate channel registry?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose includes store/read/navigate plus attach/comment/link/reference integration. The actual code implements the storage, retrieval, versioning, and navigation parts well, but the attach/comment/link/reference features are only described in the docstring, not implemented here.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make images first-class, addressable, versioned artifacts inside a larger structured ledger, so they can be referenced, compared, attached, and commented on through a common address scheme rather than ad-hoc file paths.
novelty: Distinctive combination of hierarchical image://channel/path addresses, append-only per-address versioning with @v projection, and separation of typed metadata records from content-addressed blobs, all surfaced through tree-navigation helpers.
contribution: Provides a concrete, versioned image record store with hierarchical listing and blob resolution that other ledger subsystems can reference by structured image addresses.
summary_for_embedding: runtime/cc_images.py implements a hierarchical, versioned image record store using image://channel/path addresses, persisting metadata as YAML-frontmatter markdown files and delegating binary storage to a content-addressed blob store.
intention_for_embedding: To make images first-class, navigable, versioned fabric artifacts that can be saved, retrieved, listed, and referenced consistently within a structured address ledger.

symbols.does: Exception class used for all image-operation failures.
symbols.description: A RuntimeError subclass that signals image operations could not proceed; the module uses it to enforce fail-loud behavior instead of silent no-ops.

symbols.does: Returns the current UTC timestamp as an ISO string.
symbols.description: Simple helper used to stamp created/updated times on image records.

symbols.does: Maps an image address to its on-disk markdown file path.
symbols.description: Combines IMAGES_DIR with a safe filename derived from the address, preserving the hierarchical address while using a flat storage layout.

symbols.does: Serializes an image record to YAML-frontmatter markdown.
symbols.description: Dumps whitelisted frontmatter keys with yaml.safe_dump and appends the record body after the closing frontmatter marker.

symbols.does: Parses a persisted markdown file into frontmatter dict and body string.
symbols.description: Uses lifters.frontmatter._extract plus string slicing to separate YAML metadata from the trailing body; not a full YAML frontmatter parser.

symbols.does: Normalizes input to a full image:// address.
symbols.description: Accepts either an already-qualified image:// address or a channel/path shorthand and returns a canonical image:// form.

symbols.does: Detects image MIME type from bytes and optionally validates a declared MIME.
symbols.description: Recognizes PNG, JPEG, WebP, GIF, and SVG signatures; if a declared MIME is provided it checks consistency and whitelist membership.

symbols.does: Stores an image record and its bytes, appending a new version if the address already exists.
symbols.description: Validates inputs, writes bytes to the blob store, builds an append-only version history, and atomically writes the metadata record to disk keyed by the hierarchical address.

symbols.does: Reads a single image record by address, with optional @v<n> version projection.
symbols.description: Resolves the address, loads the markdown record, and either returns the latest version or projects a specific historical version onto the record dict.

symbols.does: Returns the full version history of an image.
symbols.description: Loads the record and returns the versions list, exposing the append-only lineage for comparison or iteration.

symbols.does: Resolves an image address to its binary bytes and MIME type.
symbols.description: Looks up the record, fetches the referenced blob from the store, and returns the bytes with the record MIME.

symbols.does: Lists image records under a given prefix, or all images if no prefix.
symbols.description: Scans the images directory, filters records whose address matches the requested channel/collection prefix, and returns them newest-first.

symbols.does: Returns immediate sub-path counts within a channel.
symbols.description: Enumerates images under a channel and counts how many fall under each top-level collection, plus ungrouped and total counts.
